Lecture Transcription

oṁ namo bhagavate vāsudevāya
janmādy asya yato ’nvayād itarataś cārtheṣv abhijṣaḥ svarāṭ
tene brahma hṛdā ya ādi-kavaye muhyanti yat sūrayaḥ
tejo-vāri-mṛdāṁ yathā vinimayo yatra tri-sargo ’mṛṣā
dhāmnā svena sadā nirasta-kuhakaṁ satyaṁ paraṁ dhīmahi

[SB 1.1.1]

[O my Lord, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, son of Vasudeva, O all-pervading Personality of Godhead, I offer my respectful obeisances unto You. I meditate upon Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa because He is the Absolute Truth and the primeval cause of all causes of the creation, sustenance and destruction of the manifested universes. He is directly and indirectly conscious of all manifestations, and He is independent because there is no other cause beyond Him. It is He only who first imparted the Vedic knowledge unto the heart of Brahmājī, the original living being. By Him even the great sages and demigods are placed into illusion, as one is bewildered by the illusory representations of water seen in fire, or land seen on water. Only because of Him do the material universes, temporarily manifested by the reactions of the three modes of nature, appear factual, although they are unreal. I therefore meditate upon Him, Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, who is eternally existent in the transcendental abode, which is forever free from the illusory representations of the material world. I meditate upon Him, for He is the Absolute Truth.]

dharmaḥ projjhita-kaitavo ’tra paramo nirmatsarāṇāṁ satāṁ
vedyaṁ vāstavam atra vastu śivadaṁ tāpa-trayonmūlanam
śrīmad-bhāgavate mahā-muni-kṛte kiṁ vā parair īśvaraḥ
sadyo hṛdy avarudhyate ’tra kṛtibhiḥ śuśrūṣubhis tat-kṣaṇāt

[SB 1.1.2]

[Completely rejecting all religious activities which are materially motivated, this Bhāgavata Purāṇa propounds the highest truth, which is understandable by those devotees who are fully pure in heart. The highest truth is reality distinguished from illusion for the welfare of all. Such truth uproots the threefold miseries. This beautiful Bhāgavatam, compiled by the great sage Vyāsadeva [in his maturity], is sufficient in itself for God realization. What is the need of any other scripture? As soon as one attentively and submissively hears the message of Bhāgavatam, by this culture of knowledge the Supreme Lord is established within his heart.]

nigama-kalpa-taror galitaṁ phalaṁ
śuka-mukhād amṛta-drava-saṁyutam
pibata bhāgavataṁ rasam ālayam
muhur aho rasikā bhuvi bhāvukāḥ

[SB 1.1.3]

[O expert and thoughtful men, relish Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, the mature fruit of the desire tree of Vedic literatures. It emanated from the lips of Śrī Śukadeva Gosvāmī. Therefore this fruit has become even more tasteful, although its nectarean juice was already relishable for all, including liberated souls.]

munayaḥ sādhu pṛṣṭo ’haṁ
bhavadbhir loka-maṅgalam
yat kṛtaḥ kṛṣṇa-sampraśno
yenātmā suprasīdati

[SB 1.2.5]

[O sages, I have been justly questioned by you. Your questions are worthy because they relate to Lord Kṛṣṇa and so are of relevance to the world’s welfare. Only questions of this sort are capable of completely satisfying the self.]

sa vai puṁsāṁ paro dharmo
yato bhaktir adhokṣaje
ahaituky apratihatā
yayātmā suprasīdati

[SB 1.2.6]

[The supreme occupation [dharma] for all humanity is that by which men can attain to loving devotional service unto the transcendent Lord. Such devotional service must be unmotivated and uninterrupted to completely satisfy the self.]

vāsudeve bhagavati
bhakti-yogaḥ prayojitaḥ
janayaty āśu vairāgyaṁ
jṣānaṁ ca yad ahaitukam

[SB 1.2.7]

[By rendering devotional service unto the Personality of Godhead, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, one immediately acquires causeless knowledge and detachment from the world.]

dharmaḥ svanuṣṭhitaḥ puṁsāṁ
viṣvaksena-kathāsu yaḥ
notpādayed yadi ratiṁ
śrama eva hi kevalam

[SB 1.2.8]

[The occupational activities a man performs according to his own position are only so much useless labor if they do not provoke attraction for the message of the Personality of Godhead.]

dharmasya hy āpavargyasya
nārtho ’rthāyopakalpate
nārthasya dharmaikāntasya
kāmo lābhāya hi smṛtaḥ

[SB 1.2.9]

[All occupational engagements are certainly meant for ultimate liberation. They should never be performed for material gain. Furthermore, according to sages, one who is engaged in the ultimate occupational service should never use material gain to cultivate sense gratification.]

kāmasya nendriya-prītir
lābho jīveta yāvatā
jīvasya tattva-jijṣāsā
nārtho yaś ceha karmabhiḥ

[SB 1.2.10]

[Life’s desires should never be directed toward sense gratification. One should desire only a healthy life, or self-preservation, since a human being is meant for inquiry about the Absolute Truth. Nothing else should be the goal of one’s works.]

ataḥ pumbhir dvija-śreṣṭhā
varṇāśrama-vibhāgaśaḥ
svanuṣṭhitasya dharmasya
saṁsiddhir hari-toṣaṇam

[SB 1.2.13]

[O best among the twice-born, it is therefore concluded that the highest perfection one can achieve by discharging the duties prescribed for one’s own occupation according to caste divisions and orders of life is to please the Personality of Godhead.]

śṛṇvatāṁ sva-kathāḥ kṛṣṇaḥ
puṇya-śravaṇa-kīrtanaḥ
hṛdy antaḥ stho hy abhadrāṇi
vidhunoti suhṛt satām

[SB 1.2.17]

[Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Personality of Godhead, who is the Paramātmā [Supersoul] in everyone’s heart and the benefactor of the truthful devotee, cleanses desire for material enjoyment from the heart of the devotee who has developed the urge to hear His messages, which are in themselves virtuous when properly heard and chanted.]

naṣṭa-prāyeṣv abhadreṣu
nityaṁ bhāgavata-sevayā
bhagavaty uttama-śloke
bhaktir bhavati naiṣṭhikī

[SB 1.2.18]

[By regular attendance in classes on the Bhāgavatam and by rendering of service to the pure devotee, all that is troublesome to the heart is almost completely destroyed, and loving service unto the Personality of Godhead, who is praised with transcendental songs, is established as an irrevocable fact.]

śuśrūṣoḥ śraddadhānasya
vāsudeva-kathā-ruciḥ
syān mahat-sevayā viprāḥ
puṇya-tīrtha-niṣevaṇāt

[SB 1.2.16]

[O twice-born sages, by serving those devotees who are completely freed from all vice, great service is done. By such service, one gains affinity for hearing the messages of Vāsudeva.]

oṁ ajṣāna-timirāndhasya
jṣānāṣjana-śalākayā
cakṣur unmīlitaṁ yena
tasmai śrī-gurave namaḥ

śrī-kṛṣṇa-caitanya prabhu-nityānanda
śrī-advaita gadādhara śrīvāsādi-gaura-bhakta-vṛnda

[Mangalacharana Prayers]

hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare
hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare

We have been reciting shlokas from the first and second chapters of Srimad Bhagvatam.

dharmaḥ svanuṣṭhitaḥ puṁsāṁ
viṣvaksena-kathāsu yaḥ
notpādayed yadi ratiṁ
śrama eva hi kevalam

[SB 1.2.8]

[The occupational activities a man performs according to his own position are only so much useless labor if they do not provoke attraction for the message of the Personality of Godhead.]

This verse describes that life's desires should always be aimed at pursuing the greatest truths; that everyone even in life in order to be motivated, must establish goals, which one wants to achieve. Some people achieve wealth as a goal; some have a beautiful husband or a beautiful wife, some fame, prestige, power. Some have very simple goals- a better house, better car, good health. So everyone from the most downtrodden insignificant beggar in the street, whose goal may be to get a few spices to get his daily chapatti, for the big, big political leaders whose goal is to conquer lands and exploit people by the millions to put their name in history forever. Everyone is living according to the goals, which we established within our life. So actually, the quality of a person's life has to be determined by what we make our goal. A student has goals to have good grades, to have a career when he gets out of school. And what to do with that career?- Provide nice family, have nice house, nice car, maybe a television and a stereo, raise children, put them through school and then let them do just exactly what I'm doing. So everyone has either their simple or complex goals in life.

The Greatest Illusion In Kaliyuga

But the Garga Upanishad describes- yo va etad aksharam gargy aviditvasmal lokat praiti sa kripanah [Brihad-aranyaka Upanishad 3.8.10]: He is a miserly and unfortunate person who takes this rare human birth and quits his body, dies like the cats and the dogs without realizing the ultimate goal. Athāto brahma-jijṣāsā [Vedanta-sutra]-the ultimate goal of life is to understand what is the truth. You see, most all of society today (this is the age of Kaliyuga), is living within a great illusion and all their goals are simply part and parcel of this great illusion. Janasya moho ’yam ahaṁ mameti [SB 5.5.8]; this is the great illusion. Everyone is thinking in terms that I am this body and then all those things in relationship to this body are mine. I am Indian, I am well educated, I have this name, I am from this cast and this family is mine. These friends are mine and this home is mine, this wealth is mine, this position is mine; this is the greatest illusion. First of all, we are not this material body. This material body is a house just as we are sitting in this nice house. But soon nobody wants a guest to stay for very long [Maharaj laughs]. For a while, a guest is welcome but then it's expected that the guest will leave. Well, then we have to find another house somewhere else but we remain the same- the same identity, the same person.

So similarly, this body is the property of God. Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita; sarva-loka-maheśvaram [BG 5.29]; that everything is the property of God. So the spirit soul is temporarily residing like a guest in this body but in due course of time we're no longer wanted in this body and we have to leave. So are we this body or are we the person, the consciousness the soul that is dwelling within this body. So if I am not this body, then how can those things that are in reference to this body be mine? This is the great illusion. This is the spell of Maya in which everyone is living. And even the greatest scholars within our society cannot understand this most simple basic principle of truth. Who I am? People never even bother to ask this simple question; Who am I? Unless we understand, who am I, how will we possibly understand what is the goal of life?

Our Guru Maharaj many years back, he went in Russia, the Soviet Union. He was invited by one of the world's prominent scholars of religion, professor Kotowski, who had several PhDs. He had written textbooks, which were not only used all over the Soviet Union but all over the world in the field of Philosophy, Religion, Sociology. So one day they were having a private confidential talk and professor Kotowski spoke to our Guru Maharaj, “Swamiji, there may be so many ideas in regard to what is life and what is after death, but the fact is at the time of death everything is finished. You cease to exist.”

When Srila Prabhupada was telling us this story, it was in India and we were sitting outside. And he was pointing to the fields, where there was some very, very simple farmers and the laborers just on their hands and knees in the mud taking the rice from the paddy field.

Srila Prabhupada said that these people they have no education, these field workers- they’ve never gone to a day of school in their life. All they know is how to take the rice from the paddies. He said, “But each and every one of them, they are very careful of what they do because they know whatever they do will influence what is their reward in their next life. They understand the nature of the soul, the nature of consciousness; therefore, they are very careful in how they live.”

So who has more knowledge? These simple uneducated field workers or these big, big super famous PhD scientists and philosophers? The field workers. Because they understand the truth. Whereas these big, big scholars according to Bhagavad Gita, they are māyayāpahṛta-jṣānā [BG 7.15]. They have big, big, big, big intelligence but it is all stolen by illusion. [Maharaj laughs].

Four Types Of Men Who Never Surrender To Krishna

na māṁ duṣkṛtino mūḍhāḥ
prapadyante narādhamāḥ
māyayāpahṛta-jṣānā
āsuraṁ bhāvam āśritāḥ

[BG 7.15]

[Those miscreants who are grossly foolish, who are lowest among mankind, whose knowledge is stolen by illusion, and who partake of the atheistic nature of demons do not surrender unto Me.]

There are four types of men who never surrender to Krishna.

1.Mudhah ( Foolish )

Those who work like asses and say I just don't have time for my spiritual life. Just like some people, they work so hard and you say, “Come to pravachan, come to darshan, come to the mandir, chant the holy names of God.”

“I do not have time. I have to maintain my job, my family and everything else.”

This is called mudhah. Mudhah literally means one who is foolish like an ass. In India you have many asses. In America we hardly see animals, unless you go to the farm. But in India, even in the middle of the city you see nice asses. You know asses like donkey, they work very hard. They put huge loads on the back of this poor animal and his legs are trembling, he’s walking and they put as much as they possibly can on this little animal. And all day long he's working and foam is coming out of his mouth. Why? At the end of the day they give him a little grass and he's happy.

So similarly, people work so hard simply to maintain their physical condition of life, foaming at the mouth especially in these cities. Just to get to work sometimes you have to sit in the traffic for hours, then you work like an ass foaming at the mouth. Usually you have some man with a stick over you, beating you- do this, do this, do this, don't be late or you'll lose your job. Then you come home completely dead tired, and- “I don't have time for my spiritual life,” and then you turn on the television and try to forget your existence. This way modern civilization is going on. This is called Mudhah.

2.Naradhamah ( Lowest amongst men)

Then Naradhamaha- those who are lowest amongst men, who are just so addicted to sinful activities and crazy things that their minds are just not able to even grasp the idea that there's something beyond sense gratification.

3. Māyayāpahṛta-jṣānā (Philosophers, poets and scientists )

Then there is the Māyayāpahṛta-jṣānā- those who are very great poets, philosophers, scholars, scientists. They're very proud of their learning. But all of their intelligence is utterly stolen by illusion. Usually they are very proud.

I often use the example of one professor I met. He was so intelligent, so super intelligent and academically astute that nobody could understand a word he was saying. He had such mastery over the English language that when he would speak, every particular word was a page long and in this way he would speak. You know I'm just a simple babaji, you know, what do I know about all this scholarship. I'm sitting there listening to him and he's talking. And I'm just listening, thinking in my mind, hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare/ hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare. I don't know Hindi, everyone who knows me knows I do not know Hindi but I understand Hindi or Bengali or French or Russian or Spanish or Swahili as much as I understand this man's English. He's very proud. Then he’d asked me, “So do you understand?”

I'd say, “Yes, of course I understand.”

He said, “Well, what did I say?”

I said, “All I understand is that you are very proud of your English language. So proud that nobody could even understand what you're saying. The purpose of language is to communicate, not to show how intelligent you are.”

So I was trying to explain to him some basic simple principles about God but he couldn't grasp it. It was impossible because it was too simple for him.

In this way we have big, big scientists who come up with their theories. They write books about Big Bangs; that there was a chunk that exploded and everything was created from that explosion. And they used so many scientific procedures and vernacular to prove the fact of this Big Bang that nobody dares to challenge them.

I remember one time our Guru Maharaj was challenged by a scientist who was trying to establish just this doctrine of the Big Bang Theory; that all your religion is just sentimentalism. What is this God. It's unscientific to talk about God. I will give you the theory which closest resembles the truth of how everything was created. And he went on and on explaining all this. Prabhupada patiently sat, hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare/hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare, listened and then afterward he looked at him and he said, “My friend, I only have one simple question; who caused the Bang to go off and where did the original chunk come from?”

And man said, “There is no answer to this question.”

Srila Prabhupada said, “That is why you need a spiritual master.”

This is called māyayāpahṛta-jṣānā; big, big intelligence which simply inflates one's false ego, making one think that one is very learned but actually all is so-called learned is based on an illusion!

4. Asuraṁ bhāvam āśritāḥ ( Atheistics who defy God )

The fourth class of persons who know about God but who directly defy him and hate him because they are envious, because they want his position. Just like Hiranyakashipu; he knew that Vishnu was God but he couldn't tolerate the fact that he had to be God's servant, so he wanted to destroy God. This is the nature of the demons. Today there are very, very systematic organized movements, who are trying to destroy religion altogether. This whole idea of secular humanism is trying to replace spiritual values with mundane humanistic concepts; that just to be good and philanthropic as a human being. Let us forget all this; about the soul and of God and all this other information that we cannot see, we cannot perceive directly. In this way, in the name of doing good for humanity, they destroy devotion to God within the hearts of all. This is āsuraṁ bhāvam āśritāḥ.

So, according to the particular consciousness that we choose to pursue in our life, we also accept certain personalities who become our as we say in English, heroes. We look up to someone, we praise someone. Just like in Bombay, when you're driving down the road you see all these billboards and advertising different magazines and everyone is either glorifying or disgracing the name of some personality. Some people, they’re just mad after some movie actor or movie actress and they want to be just like him. I don't know your India so much but I know in America what its like- children, my God! Some movie actor who plays a famous role and everyone wants to be just like him. Just like when I was a young boy, there was James Bond. He's just recently coming to India and you'd go to the movie theatre, the cinema house and two-hour movie. And within that two hours, he would have sex with at least fifteen women. He would smoke at least twenty-five cigarettes. He would drink probably fifteen glasses of alcohol, he would murder, kill at least forty men- within two hours! Very talented man, James Bond.

So the young people want to be just like him. They think, my God he has the best-looking girls- fifteen in two hours! It's hard for me to get one like that in my life. I want to be just like him. So they dress like him, then they move their eyebrows like him and they hold their cigarettes like him and they walk like him and they learn to fight like him. Thinking that if I'd be like him, then I will get beautiful young girls and I will become famous and I will be very great.

And women- they see some movie actress how she's so charming and beautiful and they want to walk like her and talk like her and dress like her. This is the low class way of thinking. If you want sense gratification in hero eyes, somebody who's getting the best sense gratification and you want to be just like them. Or there are the musical entertainers, the rock-and-roll stars. Just like when some famous rock and 'roll band comes to a city in America, eighty, ninety, hundred thousand people will come to see a man just sing about sex life. They all want to be like him, they have his pictures on their clothes; they have his pictures on their walls and listen to his voice. In this way people become completely insane trying to idolize and be like such persons.

And then amongst scholars, one heroizes a famous poet; wants to model his life like that. Or a scientist; they want to be just like Charles Darwin. Or political leaders, they choose their hero. So in this way every man has to decide what is the goal of life, what do we want to pursue.

Human Life Is Especially Meant To Realize The Absolute Truth

Garga Upanishad describes as I explained earlier, that no matter how successful you are in the worldly sense, if you die without realizing your relationship with God, then your human life has been utterly wasted. Human life is specifically and exclusively meant to realize the Absolute Truth. The animals, they’re very, very expert at enjoying their senses, better than us. The natural order of civilization is not being confused and disturbed by the animal species. The ecology of the world is being destroyed by the human race. Why? Because we're living a totally unnatural condition of life. Human life is afforded by God in nature specifically for the purpose of realizing pure devotion and love in our life. If we do not use it for that purpose we will simply create chaos and disturbance to our own life, to all others and to the whole ecology around us.

Just look at the world; what man is doing in the name of modern progress is destroying the ozone, which is putting the whole planet and utter jeopardy. He's polluting the waters, polluting the air, polluting the minds. Right now they're putting nuclear waste underground in these boxes and within a few years these boxes are going to just be destroyed and soon the nuclear waste is going to go right into the earth and it's going to create radiation poisoning to practically all the crops on earth. What hopeful do we have? In the meanwhile, as the ozone is breaking down, the temperature of the planet earth is raising and they say within a few years the solar ice caps will start melting and one-fourth of the land on earth will be underwater. That means hundreds of millions of people's lives destroyed, what to speak of due to just completely uncivilized sexual habits. There are diseases like AIDS. In America they say within twenty years, one-fifth of the population of the nation will have AIDS, which means they will die a horrible death. And already cancer is growing like anything due to all the pollution in our food and in the air and in our water and in our lifestyle.

Why are all these unsolvable problems? It's all created simply because human civilization which is exclusively meant to live in devotion God is just passionately, madly trying to create unnecessary items for sense gratification under the illusion of ‘I am this body and everything in reference to this body is mine.’ The solution to the problem is truth. The problem is ignorance. The problem is an illusion. And all your honorability and all your living a very prestigious life, a good life, if it is within the illusion, you are simply a part of the problem. If you are not part of the solution, you are part of the problem. The solution is God consciousness. Therefore, whatever our activities in life, the goal should be to please Krishna.

ataḥ pumbhir dvija-śreṣṭhā
varṇāśrama-vibhāgaśaḥ
svanuṣṭhitasya dharmasya
saṁsiddhir hari-toṣaṇam

[SB 1.2.13]

[O best among the twice-born, it is therefore concluded that the highest perfection one can achieve by discharging the duties prescribed for one’s own occupation according to caste divisions and orders of life is to please the Personality of Godhead.]

So whether you are a doctor or a lawyer or a businessman or an engineer or an architect or a carpenter or a farmer or a housewife or a student or whatever field of occupation you choose to take as your life's duty, the goal is to please God.

yat karoṣi yad aśnāsi
yaj juhoṣi dadāsi yat
yat tapasyasi kaunteya
tat kuruṣva mad-arpaṇam

[BG 9.27]

[Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer or give away, and whatever austerities you perform – do that, O son of Kuntī, as an offering to Me.]

Krishna says all that you do, all that you eat, all that you offer and give away, whatever austerities you perform should be done as an offering to me. If you water the root of the tree, automatically the entire tree is nourished. Our civilization is very, very expert and well equipped at nourishing the leaves, the branches, the twigs and the bark of the tree but no one is considering the needs of the root. Therefore the whole social tree of our society is decaying. Can anyone deny that? The more they neglect the root, the longer they neglect the root, no matter how many good intentions they have, the tree of the social body decays more and more and more. The problems get worse and worse and more unsolvable and endlessly more complicated. Read the newspaper and everything I say will be confirmed on the front page.

That's why mostly people don't even like to read anything but the sports page now- the comics and the sport page are the most popular part of the newspaper because it's the only part you read and don't feel utter hopelessness after reading. If there's some hope in life, maybe the home team might win tomorrow. They lost today but they might win tomorrow. But when you read the front page you realize that no matter what we do, we're going to lose. [Laughter].

Human life is meant for the greatest attainment. Human life is meant to enjoy an eternal life full of joy, full of peace, full of bliss and full of love. It's our nature. The original consciousness of every living being is eternal, full of knowledge and full of happiness.

The Goal Of Self Realization

nitya-siddha kṛṣṇa-prema ‘sādhya’ kabhu naya
śravaṇādi-śuddha-citte karaye udaya

[CC Madhya 22.107]

[Pure love for Kṛṣṇa is eternally established in the hearts of the living entities. It is not something to be gained from another source. When the heart is purified by hearing and chanting, this love naturally awakens.]

The pure love of God is within the heart of every living being. It is our nature to love. Therefore, a spiritual life is a natural life. And real intelligence makes means to make the truth our priority in life. Krishna says in Bhagavad Gita, when you have thus learned the truth, you will know that all living things are part of me and that they are in me and that they are mine. We are meant for the pleasure of God. That is the truth. And to please God is to live the truth and everything else is a lie. How will you be happy living a lie? And at the time of death, you are going to have to face the truth after living a lie; how will you feel.

Therefore, no matter what our responsibilities are in this world, the priority and the goal of life must be devotion to Krishna, devotion to God; the pursuit of the goal of self-realization. And this truth can easily be understood through realization like chanting the transcendental sound vibration of God's holy name.

You see, this age of Kaliyuga is full of disturbances to those who seek the spiritual path. Therefore the Supreme Lord has kindly given a great benediction; that by making the chanting of the holy name the priority of our life and arrange our whole lifestyle on the basis of the devotion created by the chanting of the holy name, then our life is perfect. And there is no one with a sincere heart, that will not find time to sincerely chant God's name. When we chant the holy names, hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare/hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare or any authorized name of God, we awaken that divine consciousness that is within us. And through this process, all the good qualities of God himself awakens from our heart, because we are part of God. All the qualities of God himself are your very nature; to be self-controlled, full of bliss, full of compassion, tolerant, benevolent, charitable, merciful. These are the qualities of you; these are the qualities of every living being. The only shortage in the world is a shortage of these qualities. As I said, either you're part of the problem or you're part of the solution. It is within your integrity as a human being to decide what you want in life. No matter how old you are or how young you are, it's never too early or too late. A sincere human being will live for the truth now.

So it is the request of the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna and it is the humble request of all those great saints and sages who risk their lives tirelessly to uplift humanity. And that request is kindly make your life perfect. Find happiness and truth in your life and share it with your loved ones and give them meaning. Take the process of chanting the holy names, associate, make your heroes in life those saintly persons who are living for the sake of others. Make your joy in life tasting the sweetness of pure devotion and sharing that with others. And then the real goal, which we are all ultimately striving for, can be experienced by you. Whatever your occupation, whatever your duties; continue on. But do it for Krishna and your life will be successful.

Thank you very much. Hare Krishna

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
